content.problemStatement:
Given a binary string SSS, define f(S)=max⁡(# 0s in S, # 1s in S)f(S) = \max ( \text{\# 0s in S, \# 1s in S})f(S)=max(# 0s in S, # 1s in S), i.e. the the maximum among frequency of 000 and frequency of 111.

Find the sum of fff over all substrings of SSS. Formally, compute ∑L=1N∑R=LNf(SLSL+1...SR)\sum_{L = 1}^{N} \sum_{R = L}^{N} f(S_L S_{L + 1} ... S_R)∑L=1N​∑R=LN​f(SL​SL+1​...SR​).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the string
The second line of each test case contains SSS - the binary string.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of fff over all substrings of SSS.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 2 : There are 333 substrings:

000 : f(0)=1f(0) = 1f(0)=1
111 : f(1)=1f(1) = 1f(1)=1
010101 : f(01)=1f(01) = 1f(01)=1

Thus, the sum is 333.
